new_classes.label_en: Other wine (neither PDO/PGI nor Tokaj) in containers > 2 L and ≤ 10 L (subheading 2204 22 93)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein (weder g.U./g.g.A. noch Tokaj) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 L bis einschließlich 10 L (Unterposition 2204 22 93)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes that does not carry a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), or Tokaj designation, presented in containers of more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres. Classification is determined inter alia by total dry matter content (non-volatile substances including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts) pursuant to Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria: (1) wine of fresh grapes in containers > 2 L and ≤ 10 L, (2) absence of PDO/PGI status, (3) absence of Tokaj designation, (4) relevance of total dry matter content (non-volatile substances: sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter, salts) as required by Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, and (5) exclusion from the semi-sparkling wine subheading 2204 22 10. Coverage is not 1.0 because the specific threshold values for total dry matter content sub-categories referenced in Additional Notes 4 and 5 (which subdivide codes 22042222–22042298 into further groups) are not fully axiomatised — the ontology currently holds the quality classes but no numeric range restrictions distinguishing the exact dry-extract sub-bands. Additionally, the precise boundary between CN 2204 22 93 and its sibling 2204 22 96 (which covers the other dry-extract sub-category under "other" wines in the 2–10 L range) cannot be fully expressed without the numeric dry-extract thresholds defined in Additional Note 4A, which are referenced by the legal text but whose quantitative values are not encoded in the current TBox.
